role: Early MEK leader; first wife of Massoud Rajavi
bio: Ashraf Rabiei studied physics at Aryamehr University (now Sharif University) and joined the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) in the early 1970s. She married [Massoud Rajavi](/people/massoud-rajavi) in 1980. In the 1980 parliamentary election, she received over 300,000 votes but did not win a seat. She was killed alongside [Moussa Khiabani](/people/moussa-khiabani) on 8 February 1982 when the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) raided their safehouse in Tehran. Camp Ashraf in Iraq was named in her honor.
editorial_notes: First husband Ali-Akbar Nabavi-Nuri was killed in action in 1976.
